The gods, including the Adityas and Asuras, are born from various daughters of the Prajapati Daksha, such  ...    like Sesha and Vasuki, among others. Pradha gives birth to celestial Gandharvas and Apsaras, including Alamvusha, Tilottama, and Tumvuru, through her union with the Rishi Kasyapa. These Ratnas became early prototypes for their  ...    This Dhanteras, we shed light on two of these Ratnas and their first appearances - Goddess Lakshmi and God Dhanvantari. Srimad Bhagavata Purana states that when Goddess Lakshmi emerged from the ocean with lotus flower in her hand, the Diggajas or four elephants representing four quarters, bathed Her by pouring water from auspicious pots. It is also  ...    God Dhanvantari, the divine physician, was the one who emerged holding the pot of Amrita and the book of medicine called Ayurveda on the day of Dhanteras. Considered the Hindu God of medicine, Lord Dhanvantari is also an avatar of Lord Vishnu!
